# Domain Checklists

Load only the sections relevant to the task.

## Global Deepening Pass

When a user asks to "deepen" a topic, also read:
- [full-skill-orchestration.md](full-skill-orchestration.md)
- [first-class-system-topics.md](first-class-system-topics.md)
- [deep-dive-lenses.md](deep-dive-lenses.md)
- [topic-combination-matrix.md](topic-combination-matrix.md)

Check:
- whether the topic is being treated as a first-class system topic rather than a lore leaf
- whether the answer includes institutions, not just individuals
- whether commoners or townspeople appear, not only elites
- whether there is at least one public-space surface
- whether material life, productive rhythm, or bodily cost is visible when the topic needs it
- whether a failure mode or capture mode is named
- whether region or era flattening is being called out

## Full-Skill Integration Pass

When the user asks for the whole system, a living world, or to "string it together", also read:
- [full-skill-orchestration.md](full-skill-orchestration.md)
- [first-class-system-topics.md](first-class-system-topics.md)
- [topic-combination-matrix.md](topic-combination-matrix.md)
- [game-translation.md](game-translation.md)
- [module-mapping.md](module-mapping.md)

Check:
- whether the answer names an era baseline and a region pack or explicitly marks them as open
- whether the answer connects settlement and route ecology to household and lineage life
- whether material life, debt, storage, transport, labor, and public works are in the same world as office and ritual
- whether the answer links private force, office conflict, yamen reach, and public-order distortion rather than treating them as separate minigames
- whether public surfaces such as markets, fairs, ferries, notices, funerals, school courts, and tea houses show hidden institutional pressure
- whether death, succession, mourning, reign change, or legitimacy shock can travel across more than one layer
- whether the result ends in mechanics, boards, nodes, overlays, descriptors, or module ownership instead of stopping at explanation

Deep-dive references:
- [full-skill-orchestration.md](full-skill-orchestration.md) for the whole-chain pass from era and region through household, institutions, coercion, public life, death, and gameplay translation
- [conflict-scale-ladder.md](conflict-scale-ladder.md) for deciding whether the design should stay at pressure, local vignette, tactical-lite encounter, or campaign-board scale
- [china-ancient-sandbox-structure.md](china-ancient-sandbox-structure.md) for a more explicit Chinese-ancient sandbox layer plan, node grammar, route grammar, and desk read order
- [wargame-simulation-flow.md](wargame-simulation-flow.md) for step-by-step warfare or bingqi flow
- [force-families-differentiation.md](force-families-differentiation.md) for differentiating house, lineage, militia, official, rebel, and border force families
- [small-clash-vs-major-campaign.md](small-clash-vs-major-campaign.md) for separating local incident resolution from large campaign resolution
- [late-imperial-pack.md](late-imperial-pack.md) for a labeled late-imperial baseline of county society, yamen reach, gentry mediation, exam funnels, and grain-silver pressure
- [jiangnan-water-network-county.md](jiangnan-water-network-county.md) for a strong regional pack built around canals, ferries, docks, embankments, and water-linked county life
- [local-culture-customs-regional-identity.md](local-culture-customs-regional-identity.md) for local custom, dialect feel, foodways, outsider friction, place pride, and regional identity beyond terrain alone
- [north-china-road-county.md](north-china-road-county.md) for a dry-land county built around roads, gates, walled seats, market fairs, winter exposure, and overland notice flow
- [southwest-mountain-borderland.md](southwest-mountain-borderland.md) for mountain routes, valley markets, mixed jurisdiction, translation brokers, and upland-lowland frontier pressure
- [border-garrison-fort-belt.md](border-garrison-fort-belt.md) for forts, passes, depots, courier urgency, military households, and alert-state border life
- [simulation-calibration.md](simulation-calibration.md) for believable timing bands, settlement scale, travel pace, message delay, and response calibration
- [lineage-institutions-corporate-power.md](lineage-institutions-corporate-power.md) for ancestral halls, lineage rules, corporate assets, schools, charity, mediation, and organized clan power
- [lineage-inheritance.md](lineage-inheritance.md) for family, lineage, succession, widowhood, and inheritance pressure
- [lineage-commoner-relations.md](lineage-commoner-relations.md) for how lineages interact with tenants, dependents, retainers, clients, and ordinary households
- [lineage-private-force-retainers-conflict.md](lineage-private-force-retainers-conflict.md) for private retainers, `jiading`, estate guards, feud escalation, and armed clan conflict
- [marriage-gender-household-power.md](marriage-gender-household-power.md) for marriage, women, concubinage, domestic hierarchy, household labor, and intra-household power
- [fertility-demography-infant-mortality.md](fertility-demography-infant-mortality.md) for childbirth, infant survival, dependency waves, heir security, and uneven household reproduction
- [women-life-cycle-gendered-experience.md](women-life-cycle-gendered-experience.md) for girlhood, marriage transfer, childbearing risk, widowhood, remarriage, and elder female authority
- [disease-lifespan-death.md](disease-lifespan-death.md) for illness, aging, death, funerals, epidemics, and care burden
- [death-succession-mourning-political-shock.md](death-succession-mourning-political-shock.md) for death aftermath across household, lineage, office, command, mourning, and regime stability
- [childhood-generations.md](childhood-generations.md) for children, upbringing, sibling order, apprenticeship, elder care, and generational turnover
- [commoner-livelihoods.md](commoner-livelihoods.md) for peasant life, artisan work, petty trade, labor, migration, and everyday survival
- [property-contract-debt-credit.md](property-contract-debt-credit.md) for property claims, contracts, credit, collateral, dowry transfer, and debt dependency
- [material-life-clothing-food-housing-travel.md](material-life-clothing-food-housing-travel.md) for clothing, food, shelter, fuel, storage, ordinary travel, and visible standards of living
- [agrarian-calendar-waterworks-hazards.md](agrarian-calendar-waterworks-hazards.md) for planting and harvest rhythm, irrigation, canals, embankments, drought, flood, and agricultural hazard
- [public-works-granaries-canal-transport.md](public-works-granaries-canal-transport.md) for embankments, granaries, canals, depots, ferries, dredging, and infrastructure burden
- [crafts-guilds-workshops.md](crafts-guilds-workshops.md) for workshops, craft lanes, apprentices, tools, guild-like coordination, and repair economies
- [status-groups-military-craft-households.md](status-groups-military-craft-households.md) for military households, craft households, hereditary service obligation, and constrained mobility
- [medicine-healing-burial.md](medicine-healing-burial.md) for healers, remedies, funerals, grave upkeep, mourning, and healing uncertainty

- [route-families.md](route-families.md) for the top-level route set: social governance, lineage management, commoner household management, bandit management, commercial management, official promotion, and imperial governance
- [religion-temples-ritual-brokerage.md](religion-temples-ritual-brokerage.md) for temples, festivals, vows, spirit legitimacy, monks, priests, and local ritual brokerage
- [religion-state-and-popular-practice.md](religion-state-and-popular-practice.md) for state cult, local temples, household ritual, heterodoxy anxiety, and religion as governance texture
- [gentry-local-magnates.md](gentry-local-magnates.md) for gentry blocs, local magnates, strongmen, patrons, and estate-backed local dominance
- [household-tax-corvee.md](household-tax-corvee.md) for registration, land, tax, labor service, grain movement, and extraction burden
- [exams-offices-clerks.md](exams-offices-clerks.md) for study, exams, office access, clerks, and yamen workflow
- [education-literacy-schools-academies.md](education-literacy-schools-academies.md) for literacy, schools, academies, copying, book access, practical document competence, and educational mobility
- [official-private-interest-family-entanglement.md](official-private-interest-family-entanglement.md) for officials' household pull, kin obligation, gift exchange, clerk capture, patronage, and family-office entanglement
- [office-conflict-faction-yamen-struggle.md](office-conflict-faction-yamen-struggle.md) for office rivalry, yamen infighting, memorial attack, procedural sabotage, and factional blocking
- [imperial-power-bureaucracy-and-subjects.md](imperial-power-bureaucracy-and-subjects.md) for emperor, bureaucracy, magistrates, clerks, subjects, townspeople, and petition chains
- [imperial-sovereignty-legitimacy-succession.md](imperial-sovereignty-legitimacy-succession.md) for mandate language, reign continuity, succession clarity, amnesties, and emperor-centered legitimacy
- [imperial-local-bargaining-governance-friction.md](imperial-local-bargaining-governance-friction.md) for center-local bargaining, selective compliance, softened quotas, implementation drag, and negotiated state reach
- [court-eunuchs-outer-kin.md](court-eunuchs-outer-kin.md) for courts, eunuchs, outer-kin, favorites, succession circles, and court capture
- [law-litigation-punishment.md](law-litigation-punishment.md) for suits, mediation, adjudication, punishment, prison, and legal fear
- [body-desire-taboo.md](body-desire-taboo.md) for sexuality, scandal, vice, bodily shame, intimacy, and taboo
- [local-order-militia-banditry.md](local-order-militia-banditry.md) for local order, escorts, militias, raids, fear, and retaliation
- [military-governors-garrisons.md](military-governors-garrisons.md) for military strongmen, garrisons, regional commands, army settlements, and provincial militarization
- [secret-societies-brotherhoods.md](secret-societies-brotherhoods.md) for sworn groups, hidden networks, mutual aid rings, smuggling links, and covert organization
- [rebellion-dynasty-cycle.md](rebellion-dynasty-cycle.md) for escalation from banditry to rebellion, regime formation, dynastic legitimacy, and collapse
- [disaster-famine-relief-granaries.md](disaster-famine-relief-granaries.md) for flood, drought, famine, relief, granaries, epidemic stress, and displacement
- [city-market-town-urban-life.md](city-market-town-urban-life.md) for county seats, towns, markets, inns, workshops, wards, and urban social pressure
- [frontier-migration-settlement.md](frontier-migration-settlement.md) for migration, refugee flow, settlement, garrisons, and borderland society
- [human-motives-social-psychology.md](human-motives-social-psychology.md) for shame, fear, trust, grief, aspiration, rumor, crowd behavior, and believable motive under pressure
- [information-messaging-documents.md](information-messaging-documents.md) for rumor, letters, notices, proclamation, paperwork, and information delay
- [public-opinion-reputation-public-spaces.md](public-opinion-reputation-public-spaces.md) for crowd mood, public legitimacy, tea-house politics, public shame, and visible reputation
- [warfare-mobilization-supply-merit.md](warfare-mobilization-supply-merit.md) for campaign boards, levies, grain lines, mobilization, and merit logic
- [military-thought-and-command-culture.md](military-thought-and-command-culture.md) for bingfa, command timing, deception, terrain reading, intelligence, morale handling, and pursuit discipline
- [ancient-sandbox.md](ancient-sandbox.md) for desk-sandbox structure, social-pressure layers, and local-map verbs
- [campaign-board.md](campaign-board.md) for bingqi and warfare-lite board objects, directives, and transitions
- [china-map-prefecture-county-routes.md](china-map-prefecture-county-routes.md) for Chinese ancient state hierarchy, counties, roads, ferries, canals, and route topology

## Kinship, household, and lineage

Check:
- household versus lineage: are you modeling co-residence, descent, or corporate lineage power
- lineage versus dependent population: who belongs by blood, by tenancy, by service, or by protection
- marriage constraints, concubinage, adoption, widowhood, branch households
- inheritance and succession logic
- mourning, ancestral rites, and lineage legitimacy
- differences between elite, middling, and poor households

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- household labor pool
- lineage prestige and obligation
- inheritance pressure
- marriage alliance leverage
- ancestral legitimacy and ritual burden

## Lineage institutions and corporate clan power

Check:
- whether the lineage is only a kin graph or also an organized institution with halls, rules, records, schools, and corporate resources
- whether charity, sponsorship, mediation, discipline, and exclusion are part of clan power
- whether a strong lineage feels different from a loose kin network

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- ancestral hall weight
- corporate estate power
- lineage rule pressure
- lineage school support
- internal mediation authority

## Lineage private force, retainers, and clan conflict

Check:
- whether lineage coercive capacity is being confused with formal militia or regular troops
- whether guards, retainers, escorts, and `jiading` are tied to estate, hall, debt, feud, or prestige
- whether violence begins as intimidation, route control, revenge, or protection before open battle
- whether private force changes travel, petitioning, marriage, market confidence, and reputation

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- private force weight
- retainer loyalty
- armed household readiness
- clan conflict heat
- feud escalation
- estate coercion
- violence visibility
- retaliation depth

## Era baseline and dynasty fit

Check:
- whether the task needs an exact dynasty or only a strong labeled baseline
- whether late-imperial assumptions are being quietly projected backward
- whether tax, office, military, and city assumptions actually fit the chosen frame

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- era baseline
- dynasty fit
- institutional confidence
- late-imperial default
- specificity warning

## Region and settlement ecology

Check:
- whether the region is dry-land road oriented, water-network oriented, frontier oriented, or mixed
- whether ferries, canals, docks, passes, embankments, or market corridors change the local game surface
- whether geography is altering traffic, rumor, levy response, trade, flood risk, and escape paths
- whether the pack should read as Jiangnan water county, north-China road county, southwest mountain borderland, or border fort belt

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- route ecology
- water-route reach
- road-county pull
- crossing pressure
- flood anxiety
- market corridor density
- border alertness

## Local culture, customs, and regional identity

Check:
- whether the region feels culturally specific or only geographically reskinned
- whether dialect feel, foodways, dress norms, performance style, and customary legitimacy change how public life works
- whether outsiders, migrants, and settlers are treated differently from locals
- whether local pride, stigma, and custom shape trust, rumor, schooling, and mediation

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- local custom weight
- regional identity pull
- dialect distance
- outsider friction
- customary legitimacy
- local pride
- cultural stigma

## Calibration and playable scale

Check:
- whether travel, rumor, relief, levy, and petition timing feel believable instead of instant
- whether settlement sizes and response windows are expressed as bands rather than fake precision
- whether gameplay is clear about what is a historical estimate versus a playable placeholder

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- travel band
- message delay band
- market cadence
- muster delay
- settlement scale band

## Marriage, gender, and household power

Check:
- who controls labor, money, marriage arrangement, and ritual voice inside the household
- distinctions among wife, concubine, widow, daughter, daughter-in-law, maidservant, and matriarchal authority
- whether domestic hierarchy shapes inheritance, reputation, adoption, and household survival

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- domestic authority
- marriage leverage
- care burden
- reproductive pressure
- household voice

## Fertility, demography, and infant mortality

Check:
- whether births and child loss are changing labor horizon, heir security, and care burden rather than staying as abstract population drift
- whether child survival and maternal risk are being distinguished from simple fertility desire
- whether repeated child loss, child-heavy households, or aging households alter strategy and mood

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- fertility pressure
- infant survival
- dependency wave
- heir security
- maternal risk
- household reproduction confidence
- child-loss grief

## Women life cycle and gendered experience

Check:
- whether women are being reduced to marriage outcomes rather than appearing across girlhood, childbearing, widowhood, and elder authority
- whether natal-house ties, affinal-house pressure, and bodily risk change what power looks like
- whether a life-course view is changing labor, reputation, movement, and household voice

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- life-cycle stage
- household voice
- marriage relocation
- bodily risk
- widow autonomy
- maternal authority
- reputational exposure

## Disease, lifespan, and death

Check:
- whether sickness changes labor, debt, care, and alliance timing
- whether old age, disability, or chronic weakness alter household planning
- whether funerals, mourning, and death costs matter socially

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- illness burden
- mortality risk
- care load
- funeral cost
- continuity shock

## Death aftermath, mourning, and succession shock

Check:
- whether death is only reducing population instead of redistributing labor, care, inheritance, office continuity, and command stability
- whether mourning delays or suspends action in a socially meaningful way
- whether a household, lineage, office, army, or throne death causes different kinds of instability

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- labor vacancy
- mourning load
- succession dispute heat
- inheritance rewrite pressure
- office vacancy shock
- command loss
- public grief wave
- legitimacy afterloss

## Healing, medicine, and burial

Check:
- whether healing is being modeled as modern guaranteed treatment rather than uncertain, local, and socially mediated care
- whether funerals, burial quality, and grave maintenance shape debt, labor, reputation, and memory
- whether mourning interrupts ordinary plans instead of acting as an instant status change

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- healer access
- remedy confidence
- care expense
- burial obligation
- mourning interruption
- grave maintenance
- death memory weight

## Childhood, generations, and life stages

Check:
- whether children matter before adulthood
- whether upbringing, apprenticeship, sibling order, and elder care change long-term planning
- whether generational succession feels gradual rather than instant replacement

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- upbringing investment
- youth promise
- elder dependence
- succession horizon
- generational tension

## Material life, clothing, food, housing, and travel

Check:
- whether everyday life is only described in broad flavor words instead of visible material constraints
- whether clothing, shelter, storage, fuel, diet, and ordinary travel change dignity, survival, and mobility
- whether visible poverty or visible plenty matter for shame, rumor, marriage, and patronage

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- subsistence basket
- clothing adequacy
- shelter security
- storage quality
- fuel strain
- travel readiness
- visible poverty

## Agrarian calendar, waterworks, and hazards

Check:
- whether farming is modeled as a static yield source instead of a seasonal rhythm with labor pinch and repair duty
- whether canals, wells, embankments, drainage, drought, or flood visibly change route use, levy pain, and local legitimacy
- whether labor peaks and harvest windows alter corvee, travel, marriage timing, or market behavior

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- season labor pinch
- water control confidence
- embankment strain
- harvest window
- repair levy
- weather anxiety
- route mud drag

## Public works, granaries, and canal transport

Check:
- whether public works are doing more than decorating the map
- whether granaries, depots, canals, ferries, and repair labor alter legitimacy, transport reliability, and resentment
- whether official-priority transport and civilian movement compete in visible ways

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- works burden
- canal throughput
- granary security
- depot reliability
- transport priority
- maintenance backlog
- corvee resentment

## Commoners, livelihoods, and mobility

Check:
- whether ordinary households are only scenery or can generate pressure, requests, labor, debt, flight, and opportunity
- distinctions between smallholders, tenants, hired laborers, artisans, traders, boatmen, servants, migrants, and gray-economy actors
- seasonal survival versus accumulation
- patronage, debt, service, and local protection as access paths

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- livelihood stability
- mobility pressure
- debt dependence
- petition access
- market exposure
- route-facing opportunity

## Property, contract, debt, and credit

Check:
- whether ownership is being treated as static instead of contested, pledged, witnessed, or dependent on paperwork and custom
- whether debt is trapping future action, shame, and bargaining power instead of only reducing money
- whether land, house, tools, dowry, and grain stores are being distinguished as different asset types

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- title confidence
- contract weight
- debt service
- collateral risk
- dowry transfer
- default shame
- lender dependency

## Crafts, workshops, and guild-like coordination

Check:
- whether craft production is being collapsed into generic trade without skill chains, tools, or apprentices
- whether workshops, repair work, and town craft lanes create different pressures from farming households
- whether guild-like coordination, brokers, and order backlog matter without turning into modern trade unions

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- workshop density
- skill bottleneck
- apprentice pipeline
- tool capital
- order backlog
- guild leverage
- craft reputation

## Status groups, military households, and craft households

Check:
- whether military or craft households are only labels instead of binding service status
- whether hereditary duty changes mobility, extraction, stigma, or evasion risk
- whether different obligation groups feel materially different in labor, settlement pattern, and public identity

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- status lock
- service obligation
- exit difficulty
- hereditary burden
- state claim intensity
- evasion risk
- identity stigma

## Festivals, folkways, and entertainment

Check:
- whether the world has recurring seasonal and ritual rhythms rather than static daily sameness
- whether temple fairs, taverns, teahouses, opera, storytelling, and leisure create encounters, spending, temptation, or public visibility
- whether holidays, rites, and gift exchange shift labor, debt, and household obligation timing

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- seasonal rhythm
- festival obligation
- fair traffic
- amusement pull
- gift burden

## Religion, ritual, and temple networks

Check:
- whether temples or shrines act as legitimacy brokers, social hubs, relief nodes, or credit-like trust spaces
- whether festivals, vows, spirit fear, or ritual sponsorship change behavior
- whether monks, priests, diviners, or temple managers have brokerage power

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- ritual legitimacy
- temple influence
- vow pressure
- festival flow
- sacred brokerage

## Religion, state cult, and popular practice

Check:
- whether official recognition, local temple life, and household ritual are being incorrectly merged
- whether heterodoxy fear, omen politics, or charity reach affect governance
- whether religion changes surveillance, compliance, legitimacy, or public mood

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- state cult weight
- local temple legitimacy
- household ritual burden
- heterodoxy anxiety
- omen politics

## Gentry, magnates, and local dominance

Check:
- whether elite local actors are more than just exam winners or lineage heads
- whether estate-backed patrons, strongmen, and local brokers can bend law, tax, relief, and order
- whether local dominance sits between ordinary households and the state

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- patron web
- local dominance
- estate bloc power
- broker reach
- elite shielding

## Route families and playable scope

Check:
- whether the design has distinct top-level routes rather than one family route with cosmetic variants
- whether social governance, lineage management, commoner household management, bandit management, commercial management, official career, and imperial governance have different verbs
- whether each route changes map relevance, UI language, risks, and crossover rules
- whether late-game governance routes emerge from earlier local routes instead of appearing from nowhere
- whether these are still dynamic positions rather than fixed class lanes
- whether households and actors can shift, overlap, or be forced across layers

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- route family
- route access tier
- governance scope
- crossover pressure
- legitimacy scope
- endgame transition
- social mobility trigger
- layered role occupancy

## Social psychology and motive

Check:
- whether people act only on profit or also on shame, fear, imitation, habit, reciprocity, grief, rage, and hope
- whether rumor and crowd behavior can outrun official information
- whether household memory and local stigma alter later choices

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- face pressure
- fear memory
- trust reserve
- imitation wave
- grief burden
- aspiration pull

## Public opinion, reputation, and public spaces

Check:
- whether markets, ferries, tea-houses, temple courts, and yamen gates function as public opinion surfaces
- whether public praise, shame, notices, or proclamations affect later trust, fear, and compliance
- whether crowd mood is local and unstable rather than a single global popularity score

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- public reputation
- crowd mood
- street-talk heat
- public legitimacy
- shame exposure

## Law, litigation, and punishment

Check:
- how much people fear the yamen rather than trust it
- whether disputes are mediated, suppressed, litigated, or turned into factional leverage
- whether prison, corporal punishment, confiscation, or stigma matter socially
- whether the issue belongs to code, hall mediation, or yamen handling
- whether petition access depends on literacy, kin backing, money, brokers, or clerk capture
- whether the conflict is really about legal truth or about bargaining delay, face, or selective enforcement

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- litigation fear
- petition access
- petition friction
- adjudication delay
- clerk capture risk
- punishment severity
- confiscation risk
- stigma burden
- legal leverage
- mediation authority
- hall vs yamen jurisdiction
- face-settlement pressure

## Body, desire, and taboo

Check:
- whether sexuality, scandal, vice, and bodily shame influence alliance, violence, or reputation
- whether fertility anxiety, illicit intimacy, or bodily stigma create pressure beyond marriage contracts
- whether taboo breach produces fear, secrecy, or social punishment

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- desire pressure
- scandal heat
- bodily shame
- vice pull
- taboo breach

## Court, eunuchs, and outer-kin

Check:
- whether court politics differs from local official politics
- whether inner-palace access, eunuch networks, favorites, or outer-kin can override formal channels
- whether succession and personal access distort state decisions

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- court access
- palace brokerage
- succession heat
- favorite influence
- court capture

## Imperial power, bureaucracy, and subjects

Check:
- whether imperial power is felt only as top-level flavor instead of through magistrates, clerks, runners, notices, and punishments
- whether commoners and urban populations are being mistaken for modern citizens rather than subjects, townspeople, petitioners, and market populations
- whether magistrate capacity and clerk dependence actually shape lived state reach

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- imperial aura
- bureaucratic reach
- magistrate capacity
- clerk operational weight
- subject compliance
- petition chain friction

## Imperial sovereignty, legitimacy, and succession

Check:
- whether emperor-centered rule is being collapsed into ordinary bureaucracy
- whether accession, reign change, amnesty, mandate language, and dynastic naming alter how order is perceived
- whether succession clarity or contest changes rumor, backlog, obedience, and rebel confidence

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- throne legitimacy
- mandate confidence
- succession clarity
- court time disruption
- imperial mercy wave
- ritual center weight
- dynastic name weight
- imperial distance

## Imperial-local bargaining and governance friction

Check:
- whether imperial power is arriving as negotiated pressure rather than instant top-down execution
- whether magistrates, clerks, gentry, lineage institutions, and local brokers buffer or reinterpret orders
- whether obedience is open, paper-thin, selective, or strategically staged
- whether local actors appeal upward to imperial benevolence while resisting local extraction

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- imperial-local tension
- negotiated compliance
- paper compliance
- local buffering
- implementation drag
- broker capture
- upward appeal
- downward softening

## Education, literacy, schools, and academies

Check:
- whether education is being collapsed into exam rank instead of including literacy, copying, account keeping, petitions, and moral authority
- whether lineage schools, village schools, tutors, and academies feel institutionally different
- whether schooling cost competes with labor demand and household survival
- whether limited female literacy, practical literacy, and uneven book access shape who can move upward or resist manipulation

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- literacy rate band
- school access
- academy prestige
- book circulation
- document competence
- moral authority
- study burden
- educational mobility

## Officials, private interest, and family entanglement

Check:
- whether office holders are being treated as pure public actors rather than family-bound people under kin, debt, gift, and patronage pressure
- whether natal house, affinal house, dependents, sons' schooling, dowry, and household consumption bend official conduct
- whether clerks, retainers, and gift exchange become channels for private interest without reducing everything to cartoon corruption
- whether reputation and moral cover matter alongside greed, fear, and obligation

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- official private pull
- kin entanglement
- gift obligation
- public duty strain
- household need pressure
- clerk capture risk
- reputation cover
- lineage leverage

## Office conflict, faction, and yamen struggle

Check:
- whether conflict among officials is being flattened into clean ideology, generic corruption, or pure rank ladders
- whether memorial attack, docket control, clerk blocs, blocked promotion, and sealed-paper delay are visible
- whether county-yamen conflict is being distinguished from court-level faction
- whether office conflict visibly distorts tax, justice, relief, punishment, and public order

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- office faction heat
- docket capture
- promotion block
- procedural freeze
- memorial attack risk
- clerk split
- patron backing
- administrative poison

## Office, education, and examinations

Check:
- whether the era implies recommendation, schooling, examinations, purchase, hereditary office, clerical service, or mixed pathways
- distinction between degree holders, office holders, clerks, and local strongmen
- educational bottlenecks, sponsorship, lineage schooling, and patronage

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- access funnels
- prestige conversion
- office risk and duty
- exam uncertainty
- patron-client dependency

## Military thought and command culture

Check:
- whether bingfa is being flattened into unit tactics instead of timing, deception, terrain reading, intelligence, morale, and command temperament
- whether military thought changes campaign posture and risk appetite
- whether terrain and information matter as route logic and confidence rather than only static bonuses

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- timing advantage
- deception window
- terrain read
- intelligence confidence
- morale handling
- command temperament

## Conflict scale and battle layering

Check:
- whether the design is forcing every violent event into a campaign board when some should stay as pressure or local vignette
- whether local clashes, escort fights, feud violence, riot suppression, and yamen incidents are distinguished from multi-settlement campaigns
- whether tactical-lite resolution is used only when posture, terrain, cohesion, and rout risk matter
- whether conflict outcomes fall back into household, order, route, office, and legitimacy systems

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- engagement scale
- force family
- force posture
- terrain edge
- cohesion state
- rout risk
- civilian exposure
- aftermath spread

## China-ancient sandbox structure

Check:
- whether the sandbox has clear administrative, household, material, public-life, and conflict layers
- whether node grammar is based on county logic rather than decorative map clutter
- whether route grammar includes tax, grain, petition, escort, exam, market, and military movement routes
- whether the player can read pressure in a stable order from node to route to actor to public surface to intervention

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- node grammar
- route grammar
- overlay family
- desk read order
- intervention surface

## Wargame simulation flow

Check:
- whether warfare resolution starts with scale choice and situation read instead of instant battle
- whether command intent, force commitment, route commitment, contact, clash, and aftermath are visibly distinct stages
- whether non-contact outcomes such as deterrence, withdrawal, or route opening are allowed

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- command intent
- force commitment
- route commitment
- contact outcome
- clash outcome
- aftermath return

## Force families differentiation

Check:
- whether forces are being separated by authorization, support, mobility, hold capacity, discipline, legitimacy, and fallout rather than only by weapons
- whether `jiabing`, `jiading`, `tuanlian`, yamen force, regular detachments, rebel bands, and garrison force feel socially different
- whether the chosen force families map cleanly into local pressure, local clash, or campaign use

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- authorization source
- support source
- mobility band
- hold capacity
- discipline band
- public legitimacy band
- fallout profile

## Small clash versus major campaign

Check:
- whether one-node incidents and multi-route operations are being kept distinct
- whether small clashes produce vignette-scale results and major campaigns produce board-scale results
- whether both layers feed aftermath back into the living world

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- conflict scale
- incident scope
- front scope
- node stress
- route stress
- local aftermath
- campaign aftermath

## Land, tax, labor, and trade

Check:
- household registration and who is visible to the state
- rent, corvee, grain, silver, transport, brokerage, debt
- market-town reach, merchant intermediaries, guild-like coordination, itinerant trade
- regional and period variation in tax logic

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- taxable household pressure
- land security versus liquidity
- market access
- transport friction
- debt dependence

## Property regimes and obligation locks

Check:
- whether contract, credit, and status-bound households are changing who can move, borrow, evade, inherit, or be claimed by the state
- whether military and craft obligations sit inside the same county economy as land, rent, and transport instead of in a detached category
- whether burdens feel inheritable, negotiated, or escapable in different ways

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- claim density
- obligation lock
- hereditary service burden
- contract leverage
- release difficulty
- extraction layering

## Disaster, famine, and relief

Check:
- whether flood, drought, crop failure, epidemic, or cold shock change survival and legitimacy
- whether granaries, relief, temple aid, private charity, or predatory buying appear
- whether disaster triggers migration, banditry, family breakup, or revolt

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- subsistence shock
- granary trust
- relief reach
- displacement pressure
- opportunistic extraction

## City, market town, and urban life

Check:
- whether county seats, market towns, inns, workshops, and warehouse zones behave differently from villages
- whether urban pressure creates clerk, craft, broker, lodging, vice, or policing gameplay
- whether consumption and information move differently in town space

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- town pull
- urban crowding
- workshop density
- market rumor flow
- lodging traffic

## Order, banditry, and coercion

Check:
- who actually enforces order: yamen, runners, elders, militias, retainers, private force
- difference between formal suppression and negotiated coexistence
- how rumor, reputation, and kin protection shape enforcement

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- local coercive capacity
- enforcement delay
- clan shielding
- fear and retaliation memory

## Military governors, garrisons, and army provinces

Check:
- whether military regions begin acting like semi-independent power bases
- whether garrisons feed settlement, coercion, extraction, and local dependency
- whether military command and civil rule drift apart

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- garrison grip
- regional command autonomy
- army-settlement pull
- military extraction
- provincial fracture

## Secret societies, brotherhoods, and covert networks

Check:
- whether hidden organization overlaps with trade, transport, protection, religion, or rebellion
- whether sworn groups provide trust where formal order is weak
- whether covert ties let actors move goods, people, or rumors outside formal channels

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- hidden trust
- sworn loyalty
- covert reach
- smuggling corridor
- shadow mobilization

## Conflict and warfare

Check:
- campaign versus skirmish scope
- recruitment base, supply, desertion, command fragmentation
- frontier versus interior conditions
- whether titles, units, and command structures are era-specific
- whether local disorder can escalate into rebel governance, regional fracture, regime claim, and dynastic turnover

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- manpower quality
- logistics ceiling
- loyalty fragmentation
- campaign momentum
- aftermath on households and memory

## Rebellion, polity, and dynasty cycle

Check:
- what converts bandit pressure into ideological, fiscal, or survival rebellion
- how families, retainers, clients, and local officers choose sides
- how a rebel force starts governing rather than merely raiding
- what produces legitimacy claims: grain relief, protection, mandate rhetoric, office grants, ritual forms, dynastic naming
- what later produces dynastic decay: tax exhaustion, military overreach, succession crisis, court capture, route fracture, local strongmen

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- escalation ladder
- side-taking pressure
- rebel governance capacity
- legitimacy claim
- dynastic fatigue
- collapse momentum

## Frontier, migration, and settlement

Check:
- whether migration is forced, opportunistic, punitive, or strategic
- whether frontier zones change land access, violence, mixed populations, and governance reach
- whether settlement offers opportunity together with weak ritual anchoring and high security risk

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- frontier pull
- settlement insecurity
- refugee flow
- mixed-population tension
- colonization opportunity

## Information, messaging, and documents

Check:
- whether rumor outruns official notice
- whether letters, yamen documents, proclamations, and market gossip have different authority
- whether information delay and distortion create bad decisions, panic, or advantage

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- message delay
- rumor heat
- documentary weight
- verification cost
- information asymmetry

## Naming, terminology, and voice

Check:
- surname, given name, style name, title, office, kinship term, and honorific are not interchangeable
- title wording should match rank and relationship
- avoid modern bureaucratic or pop-historical phrasing
- if using Chinese terms, provide a gloss when precision matters

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- household-facing plain labels in UI
- precise Chinese-facing descriptors in narrative and docs
- glossary-backed terminology for repeated concepts

## Influence, power, and factional pull

Check:
- whether the design is flattening all power into one prestige score
- whether lineage prestige, favor debt, office authority, wealth pull, coercive weight, public legitimacy, and information reach are being distinguished
- whether power blocs are temporary and overlapping rather than eternal fixed factions
- whether the answer can say who can delay, shield, shame, sponsor, block, or accelerate another actor
- whether factional pull is changing marriage, law, office, debt, conflict, or local order rather than staying decorative

Good abstractions for Zongzu:
- lineage prestige
- office leverage
- favor debt
- petition access
- broker reach
- faction heat
- shielding capacity
- public legitimacy
- intimidation profile
- backlash risk
